target: CAT-005 Recommended Proposal Package — Refraction Cascade (revision 2, addressing 7 curmudgeon blockers)

B1_per_win_audit.detail: All 7 flagged WINs re-read from wins.json. Corrections below.

WIN-010.actual_topic: BOU 2017 eclipse -10.9 nT magnetic anomaly (NOT analemma/solar as assumed)
WIN-010.corrected_proposal: Stay at Std Model Explains. CASCADE REMOVED from WIN-010. Eclipse magnetic effects are explained by Chapman ionospheric mechanism (Meza et al. 2021). The cascade Point 6 (eclipse geometry paradox) could apply in principle — eclipse shadow positions require a definite physical sun altitude — but WIN-010 is about magnetic MAGNITUDE, not shadow POSITION. The Chapman mechanism operates regardless of whether the dome specifies one or two sun altitudes. Cascade is not the strongest argument here; Chapman is.

WIN-016.actual_topic: Aberration refractive model — alpha = 2.56e-8 reproduces 20.5 arcsec aberration
WIN-016.corrected_proposal: Stay at Refuted by Data. Dual label WITHDRAWN per B3. Gaia/VLBI refutation is decisive. Add one-sentence cascade supplementary note: dome n(r) at stellar radii would displace all star positions by degrees, independently confirming the data refutation.

WIN-017.actual_topic: Parallax = firmament wobble (20m lateral wobble produces 0-0.5 arcsec parallax, NOT triangulation)
WIN-017.corrected_proposal: Stay at Refuted by Data per B2. Gaia 1.8B star parallax measurements are a data kill — this is the strongest category for this WIN. The cascade adds a supplementary note (n(r) wobble would affect all observations, not just parallax), but does not justify downgrading from Refuted by Data.

WIN-018.actual_topic: Analemma day length 6.9 min (RMS of obliquity sub-component only)
WIN-018.corrected_proposal: KEEP flip to Self-Contradicted (moderate confidence). The 6.9 min figure depends on the equation of time, which requires a specific solar orbit. The cascade seasonal altitude variation (1975-3618 km) changes the effective solar orbit radius seasonally by ~83%, which would modulate the equation of time far beyond the observed 6.9 min component. However: the existing Misleading verdict (cherry-picked sub-statistic with no dome derivation) is also valid. Flip adds cascade as PRIMARY; cherry-pick becomes secondary.
WIN-018.verdict_change: Misleading → Self-Contradicted (moderate confidence, curmudgeon to validate)

WIN-019.actual_topic: Analemma loop ratio 2.66 (spirograph gear-ratio parameter 8/3, NOT a measured observable)
WIN-019.corrected_proposal: Stay at Misleading. The 2.66 loop ratio is a dome epicyclic parameter, not derived from sun altitude. The cascade altitude variation would affect the analemma SHAPE but the current argument (2.66 matches no measurable property) is more devastating. Cascade adds weak supplementary note at best.

WIN-026.actual_topic: Crepuscular ray divergence from local sun at ~5733 km (refuted by anticrepuscular convergence)
WIN-026.corrected_proposal: Stay at Refuted by Data per B2. Anticrepuscular convergence at the anti-solar point is physically impossible with a local sun — this is an observational fact, not a geometric model argument. The cascade is irrelevant: crepuscular ray geometry depends on the APPARENT sun position, not the physical one, and the cascade does not change the apparent position (it IS 5733 km by definition). If anything, the cascade makes the dome argument slightly BETTER for crepuscular rays by keeping the apparent altitude fixed.

WIN-068.actual_topic: Eclipse magnetic ensemble (39 eclipses, 207 manifestations, 100 INTERMAGNET stations)
WIN-068.corrected_proposal: Keep as MODERATE flip candidate. Eclipse shadow predictions require knowing the physical sun position. If the cascade makes the physical altitude seasonally variable (1975-3618 km) and the dome has no equation to specify it at eclipse time, the 39-eclipse correlation becomes geometrically underdetermined. However: Chapman mechanism (1933) already explains the magnetic signal as ionospheric conductivity reduction, and that explanation does not depend on precise sun altitude. Trade-off: Self-Contradicted (geometry) vs Std Model Explains (Chapman). Both are strong. Curmudgeon editorial call.
WIN-068.verdict_change: Std Model Explains → Self-Contradicted (moderate, curmudgeon decides)

B1_per_win_audit.summary: After per-WIN re-read: 5 of 7 flagged WINs stay put (WIN-010, WIN-016, WIN-017, WIN-019, WIN-026). 2 remain as moderate flip candidates (WIN-018, WIN-068). This significantly reduces the flip table from 12 to fewer actual flips.

B2_refuted_by_data_protection.detail: WIN-017 and WIN-026 both stay at Refuted by Data. No downgrades proposed. Cascade adds supplementary cross-reference only.

B3_dual_label_withdrawn.detail: WIN-016 dual-label proposal withdrawn. Verdict stays Refuted by Data. Cascade noted as supplementary evidence in detail text only.

B4_numerics_verification.detail: EXP-050-numerics.json produced with computed values. Key corrections to original claims: (1) refraction angle range is 6.3-29.0 deg (wider than stated 13-27, but 13-27 is accurate for winter at 5000-15000 km observer distances); (2) ratio vs atmospheric refraction is 11-51x vs horizon, 210-967x vs zenith (original 400-675x was conflating zenith comparison at specific distances); (3) n(r) starlight bending claim of 0.04 deg was WRONG — dome n(r) at r=6700km gives n=1.235, implying multi-degree bending inconsistent with Gaia microarcsecond precision; (4) 83.2% angular diameter modulation VERIFIED; (5) 6.1x tidal modulation VERIFIED (within original 3-8x bracket).

B5_cascade_dependent_tag_dropped.detail: cascade_dependent code_analysis tag DROPPED per curmudgeon direction. Cascade-affected WINs will be identified by prose cross-references to Section 1.8 in their detail_verdict_text. No schema change needed.

B6_structural_title.detail: Title changed to: "1.8 The Refraction Cascade: When One Fix Breaks Everything" — structural, not version-coupled. If V52+ renames the split, the title survives because it describes the cascade pattern, not the version.

B7_section_number.detail: Section number changed from 1.7 to 1.8 to avoid collision with Section 1.7 (The Gravity Paradox).

revised_verdict_flip_table.method_note: Revised after per-WIN re-read of all 12 affected WINs. Significantly more conservative than v1.

rows.reason: Eclipse magnetic WIN — Chapman mechanism is the right argument. Cascade irrelevant.

Gaia 1.8B parallax kill. Add cascade supplementary note.
Equation of time depends on solar orbit; cascade altitude variation breaks it. Cherry-pick argument retained as secondary.
2.66 is a gear-ratio parameter, not altitude-dependent. Cascade irrelevant.
Anticrepuscular convergence is observational. Cascade irrelevant (concerns apparent altitude, which is unchanged).
Cascade refraction directly contradicts claim. Clean structural argument.
Already correct verdict. Add Section 1.8 cross-reference.
Anchor WIN for cascade section. Elevate prominence.
Data kill stronger. Add cascade supplementary note.
Eclipse geometry paradox. Curmudgeon editorial call — Chapman is also strong.

summary.flip_details: 2 moderate (018, 068) + 1 strong (044). Much more conservative than v1 (which proposed 10 flips).
summary.net_change_to_verdict_tallies: If all 3 flips accepted: Self-Contradicted +3, Misleading -1, Std Model Explains -1, net shift of 2-3 WINs between categories.

revised_section_proposal.title: 1.8 The Refraction Cascade: When One Fix Breaks Everything
revised_section_proposal.placement: Part 1 (The Model tab), after Section 1.7 (The Gravity Paradox)
revised_section_proposal.outline_unchanged: Same 7-point structure as v1. Numerics now verified in EXP-050-numerics.json. Updated range claims: refraction angle 6-29 deg (typical 13-27 at mid-range in winter), ratio vs atmosphere 11-51x horizon / 200-1000x zenith, n(r) structural inconsistency (qualitative, not quantitative 400-675x), 83% angular modulation verified, 6.1x tidal modulation verified.
revised_section_proposal.prose_writing: Deferred to separate EXP item after curmudgeon gate 2 passes.
